task_completion.description: Verify the Tor Browser Security Level is set to 'Safest'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the Tor Browser settings page (Privacy & Security section) or the Shield icon dropdown menu showing the 'Safest' option selected.

task_completion.description: Verify HTTPS-Only Mode is enabled for all windows.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the Tor Browser Privacy & Security settings page showing the 'Enable HTTPS-Only Mode in all windows' radio button selected.

task_completion.description: Verify the browser is configured to never remember history.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the Tor Browser Privacy & Security settings page under the History section, showing the dropdown set to 'Never remember history'.
Look for the 'about:config' page showing the preference 'network.prefetch-next' with its value set to 'false'.
Look for the 'about:config' page showing the preference 'browser.sessionstore.privacy_level' with its value set to '2'.
Look for the 'about:config' page showing the preference 'network.http.speculative-parallel-limit' with its value set to '0'.

integrity.description: Verify the agent interacted with the actual Tor Browser application.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the genuine Tor Browser UI (e.g., onion logo, Tor-specific settings, about:config interface) rather than a text editor modifying prefs.js or terminal commands.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the graphical user interface to change settings.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for screenshots of the about:preferences and about:config pages being actively navigated and modified, rather than terminal outputs or scripts.

integrity.description: Verify the settings were genuinely applied and not just typed into a document or spoofed.
integrity.visual_evidence: The fonts, alignment, and UI elements in about:config and about:preferences should match standard Firefox/Tor Browser rendering without signs of image manipulation or HTML DOM spoofing.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target security level is 'Safest'. In the Tor Browser UI, this is verified visually via the Shield icon or about:preferences. (Note: internally this corresponds to a security_slider value of 1, not 4). The required about:config preferences are: network.prefetch-next=false, browser.sessionstore.privacy_level=2, and network.http.speculative-parallel-limit=0. HTTPS-Only Mode must be enabled for all windows, and History must be set to 'Never remember history'.
